title: timestamp selection based on now() takes too long
poc: CREATE TABLE "sensor_readings" ( "src_ip" VARCHAR(15), "recv_time" TIMESTAMP, "emit_time" TIMESTAMP, "location" VARCHAR(30), "type" VARCHAR(30), "value" VARCHAR(30) );
select count(*) from sensor_readings where emit_time >= '2010-09-28 13:16:09.000000+00:00';
select count(*) from sensor_readings where emit_time >= now() - interval '30' second;
select count(*) from sensor_readings where emit_time >= ts;
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE sensor_readings (src_ip VARCHAR(15), recv_time TIMESTAMP, emit_time TIMESTAMP, location VARCHAR(30), type VARCHAR(30), value VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO sensor_readings (src_ip, recv_time, emit_time, location, type, value) VALUES ('192.168.1.1', now(), now() - interval '10' minute, 'LocationA', 'Type1', 'Value1');
INSERT INTO sensor_readings (src_ip, recv_time, emit_time, location, type, value) VALUES (NULL, now(), now() - interval '5' minute, 'LocationB', 'Type2', 'Value2');
SELECT count(*) FROM sensor_readings WHERE emit_time >= now() - interval '1' hour;
SELECT max(emit_time) FROM sensor_readings WHERE emit_time IS NOT NULL;
